_meta.last_reviewed: 2026-05-04 (Plan A4 — added 35 cofactor/antagonism/timing/risk pairings, 10 depletions, 8 dangerous)
_meta.source: data.js (733 supplements; mod date 2026-04-24, no net add/remove vs prior 733 count)
_meta.notes: Each pairing references supplement `n` (name) strings as they appear in `data.js`. `kind` describes WHY the pair belongs together; `strength` is 1-5 (5 = mandatory for the effect, 1 = speculative). `apart_from` lists things the supplement competes with for absorption.
changelog: v1.3 (2026-05-01): Full-database audit. Renamed 30 orphan member references to canonical supplement names; added 25 new pairings covering methylation, mineral interactions, sleep stack, BP/glucose synergies, depression adjuncts, and absorption boosters.
v1.1 (2026-04-23 PM): Added 25 additional pairings (p31-p55) surfaced from a complete re-read of data.js prose. All existing pairings (p1-p30) remain unchanged.

v1.2 (2026-04-25): Added 7 additional pairings (p56-p62) surfaced from data.js entries modified 2026-04-24 — chiefly explicit stack entries (Adaptogen stack, HA+Chondroitin stack), prose synergies (Casein vs Whey timing, Theacrine+Caffeine, Beta-alanine+Creatine, Proline+C+Glycine), and the Dan Shen/Sanqi TCM pair. Existing p1-p55 unchanged.

pairings.rationale: D3 increases intestinal Ca absorption. K2 activates osteocalcin/MGP, directing Ca into bone rather than arteries.
Calcium + D3 reduces hip fracture risk; K2 ensures deposition into bone.
pairings.dose: Calcium 500-600 mg/dose + D3 1,000-2,000 IU + MK-7 90-200 mcg.

pairings.rationale: Ascorbate reduces Fe3+ to Fe2+ and chelates it for uptake. Increases absorption 2-3x.
pairings.dose: 25-50 mg elemental Fe (bisglycinate) + 100-250 mg C on empty stomach.

pairings.rationale: Vitamin C is a required cofactor for prolyl and lysyl hydroxylases in collagen cross-linking.
pairings.dose: 10-15 g collagen + 50 mg C. For tendon rehab, 60 min pre-exercise (Shaw protocol).

pairings.rationale: Theanine raises alpha-wave activity and blunts caffeine-driven HR/anxiety; RCT-backed 2:1 ratio.
pairings.dose: 200 mg theanine + 100 mg caffeine. Morning. No afternoon caffeine.

pairings.goal: Prevent copper deficiency from long-term zinc use
pairings.rationale: Zn blocks Cu absorption via metallothionein induction. Long-term Zn >25 mg/day depletes Cu and can cause sideroblastic anemia and myelopathy.
pairings.dose: Zn 25-30 mg + Cu 1-2 mg, taken at different meals.

pairings.rationale: Piperine inhibits hepatic glucuronidation, raising serum curcumin up to 20x. Not needed if using phytosomal or liposomal forms.
pairings.dose: Curcuminoids 250-500 mg + piperine 5 mg with fat.

pairings.goal: Gut colonisation and short-chain fatty acid production
pairings.rationale: Probiotic strains require prebiotic substrate (inulin, acacia, psyllium, resistant starch) to establish and produce SCFAs. Synbiotic concept.
pairings.dose: 1-10B CFU strain-specific + 5-10 g soluble fibre daily.

pairings.rationale: The two feed the same cycle via methionine synthase. High-dose folate masks B12 deficiency and can accelerate neurologic damage.
pairings.dose: 400-800 mcg 5-MTHF + 500-1,000 mcg methylcobalamin.

pairings.goal: PCOS: ovulation, insulin sensitivity, androgen reduction
pairings.rationale: Ovarian tissue uses a ~40:1 myo:DCI ratio. High-dose DCI alone worsens oocyte quality (DCI paradox).
pairings.dose: 2,000 mg myo + 50 mg DCI (40:1), twice daily.
pairings.warning: Do not take standalone high-dose DCI for fertility.

pairings.rationale: Lutein and zeaxanthin concentrate in different macular regions. AREDS2 formula combines them.
pairings.dose: 10 mg L + 2 mg Z with fat.
pairings.existing_entry: Lutein + Zeaxanthin (single combined entry in data)

pairings.rationale: Racetams upregulate ACh turnover; added choline prevents the choline-depletion headache.
pairings.dose: Racetam as prescribed + 300-600 mg alpha-GPC or 250 mg CDP-choline.

pairings.rationale: Mg is a cofactor for hepatic 25-hydroxylation and renal 1-alpha hydroxylation of D3. Low Mg = low active D even with adequate dose.
pairings.dose: Mg glycinate 200-400 mg + D3 1,000-2,000 IU.

pairings.rationale: Pea is low in Met/Cys; rice is low in Lys. Together they rival whey's leucine / BCAA profile.
pairings.dose: 20-40 g combined, ~70:30 pea:rice.

pairings.goal: Maximal muscle protein synthesis in older adults
pairings.rationale: Per-meal leucine must exceed ~2.5 g to trigger mTORC1. Adults over 65 have anabolic resistance and often need 3-4 g.
pairings.dose: 20-30 g protein + 2-3 g leucine per meal.

pairings.rationale: Most omega-3 products already include tocopherol as antioxidant, but high-dose EPA/DHA users benefit from ~15-30 mg mixed tocopherols.
pairings.dose: EPA/DHA 1-4 g + 15-30 mg mixed tocopherols.

pairings.rationale: PQQ appears to promote mitochondrial biogenesis while CoQ10 acts as an electron carrier. Data entry notes the synergy is commonly cited; human trial data for the combination is limited.
pairings.dose: Ubiquinol 100-200 mg + PQQ 10-20 mg with fat.

pairings.rationale: Each has independent RCT evidence for ~2-day reduction in cold duration when started <48 h from onset.
pairings.dose: 15 mL elderberry + 75-92 mg Zn (lozenges) for up to 5 days.

pairings.goal: Modest cartilage and joint-pain protection in moderate osteoarthritis
pairings.rationale: GAIT trial found the combination outperformed either component alone in the moderate-to-severe pain subgroup.
pairings.dose: Glucosamine sulfate 1,500 mg + chondroitin sulfate 800-1,200 mg.

pairings.rationale: Boswellic acids inhibit 5-LOX; curcumin inhibits NF-kB and COX-2. Non-overlapping pathways.
pairings.dose: Boswellia 300 mg + curcumin 500 mg with fat.

pairings.rationale: Garlic modestly reduces BP and LDL; omega-3 addresses triglycerides and inflammation.
pairings.dose: AGE 600-1,200 mg + EPA/DHA 1-2 g.

pairings.rationale: Classic European bioflavonoid + C formula for chronic venous insufficiency.
pairings.dose: Hesperidin 500 mg + rutin 500-1,000 mg + C 500 mg.

pairings.rationale: Both are expectorants; RCTs support the combination for cough frequency and duration.
pairings.dose: Ivy 300 mg + thyme 300-600 mg.

pairings.rationale: NR raises NAD+ substrate; pterostilbene activates sirtuins that use it. Basis protocol raised NAD+ and improved liver markers; clinical longevity endpoints unproven.
pairings.dose: NR 250 mg + pterostilbene 50 mg.

pairings.goal: Iron repletion with fewer GI side-effects
pairings.rationale: Lactoferrin binds iron for controlled release, improving hemoglobin response with less GI upset than ferrous sulfate.
pairings.dose: Lactoferrin 100-250 mg + ferrous bisglycinate 25-50 mg.

pairings.rationale: NAC provides the rate-limiting cysteine; glycine is the other key substrate. The GlyNAC concept shows improved redox in aging pilots.
pairings.dose: NAC 600-1,200 mg + glycine 3 g.

pairings.goal: Nitric oxide bioavailability for endurance / blood pressure
pairings.rationale: Citrulline via the L-arginine / NOS pathway; nitrate via the nitrate-nitrite-NO pathway. Additive in some small trials.
pairings.dose: Citrulline 6-8 g + beetroot 400-500 mg nitrate, 2-3 h pre-exercise.

pairings.goal: Blood pressure and cardiac rhythm support
pairings.rationale: Mg-taurate already pairs the two, but adding extra taurine (1-3 g) may provide additive cardiac support in some patients.
Creatine builds PCr stores and supports strength; HMB suppresses proteolysis. Each has independent evidence; head-to-head combo trials are thin.
pairings.dose: Creatine 3-5 g + HMB 3 g daily.

pairings.rationale: Builds on the WHI Ca+D trial; adds Mg (cofactor), K2 (bone deposition), and optional boron for matrix support.
pairings.dose: Ca 500 mg x2/day + Mg 200-400 mg + D3 1,000-2,000 IU + MK-7 90-200 mcg + boron 3 mg.

pairings.goal: Bone density via food-form calcium matrix
pairings.rationale: MCHC carries calcium plus native collagen/phosphate; still requires D3 for absorption and K2 for bone-deposition routing. Variant of p2/p30 for people using whole-bone calcium sources.
pairings.dose: MCHC 1,000 mg/day + D3 1,000-2,000 IU + MK-7 90-200 mcg, with food.
pairings.source_field: desc/tips on Calcium hydroxyapatite entry

pairings.rationale: UMP + DHA + choline together supply all three substrates for phosphatidylcholine in neurons (Wurtman/MIT work, Souvenaid trials). UMP alone is weakly supported; the combination is the evidenced form.
pairings.dose: UMP 250-500 mg + DHA 500-1,000 mg + Alpha-GPC 300 mg or CDP-choline 250 mg.
pairings.source_field: UMP entry: 'synergistic effect with DHA is better evidenced than standalone'

pairings.goal: Gut barrier / short-chain fatty acid support
pairings.rationale: Tributyrin delivers butyrate to the colon; probiotics + prebiotic fibre also produce butyrate via fermentation. Layering a direct donor with substrate-driven production gives continuous SCFA coverage.
pairings.dose: Tributyrin 300-600 mg + 1-10B CFU probiotic + 5-10 g soluble fibre.
pairings.source_field: Tributyrin entry: 'Can be combined with probiotics for synergistic gut barrier support'

pairings.rationale: TMG provides methyl groups via the BHMT pathway in liver/kidney; 5-MTHF + B12 drive the methionine synthase pathway everywhere else; B6 supports the transsulfuration branch. Together they cover both arms of homocysteine clearance.
pairings.dose: TMG 1,000-3,000 mg + 5-MTHF 400-800 mcg + methyl-B12 500 mcg + P5P 25-50 mg.
pairings.source_field: TMG entry: 'Safe to combine with B12 and folate' + Choline bitartrate entry: 'Works synergistically with B12 and folate'

pairings.goal: GlyNAC extended — glutathione synthesis + transsulfuration support
pairings.rationale: Extends p26. L-cysteine/NAC + glycine are the two limiting substrates for glutathione; P5P and Zn are cofactors for the transsulfuration enzymes (CBS, CSE) that produce endogenous cysteine.
pairings.dose: NAC 600-1,200 mg + glycine 3 g + P5P 25 mg + Zn 15-25 mg (with Cu 1-2 mg if long-term).
pairings.source_field: L-Cysteine entry: 'Synergistic with glycine (GlyNAC stack); add B6 and zinc to support the transsulfuration pathway'

pairings.goal: Prevent arachidonic-acid buildup from high-dose GLA
pairings.rationale: GLA can be elongated to arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory) if the pathway is not balanced by EPA/DHA intake. This pairing is more than convenience — it is a safety-relevant balance.
pairings.dose: Borage oil 500-1,000 mg GLA + EPA/DHA 1-2 g.
pairings.source_field: Borage oil entry: 'combine with fish oil to prevent arachidonic-acid buildup from GLA'

pairings.rationale: Overlapping but non-identical effects on mast cells, NF-kB, and senescent cells. Kaempferol and luteolin entries both explicitly cite quercetin; fisetin has the strongest senolytic evidence.
pairings.dose: Quercetin 500 mg + luteolin 100 mg + kaempferol 50 mg + fisetin 100 mg daily (or fisetin 1,000 mg x2 days/month pulse).
pairings.source_field: Kaempferol entry: 'broader flavonoid longevity stack'; Luteolin entry: 'synergistic with quercetin'

pairings.rationale: GOS selectively feeds bifidobacteria — a specific case of p8 with stronger trial data than generic fibre for raising Bifidobacterium counts.
pairings.dose: GOS 3.5-5 g + Bifidobacterium-containing probiotic 1-10B CFU.
pairings.source_field: GOS entry: 'highly synergistic with probiotic supplementation'

pairings.rationale: HMOs and GOS feed overlapping but distinct Bifidobacterium species; combining broadens coverage closer to the breast-milk substrate pattern.
pairings.dose: 2'-FL 1-3 g + GOS 3-5 g + probiotic 1-10B CFU.
pairings.source_field: 2'-FL entry: 'combined with other HMOs or GOS for broader prebiotic coverage'

pairings.goal: Complete oat matrix — cholesterol + vascular antioxidant
pairings.rationale: Beta-glucan handles LDL via bile-acid sequestration; avenanthramides add endothelial antioxidant activity. Whole oats deliver both; isolated supplementation loses one.
pairings.dose: Beta-glucan 3-10 g + avenanthramides (dose not standardised — whole oat sources preferred).
pairings.source_field: Avenanthramides entry: 'synergistic with oat beta-glucan for complete oat benefits'

pairings.rationale: HBCD supplies rapid low-osmolality carbs; EAAs supply amino-acid substrate without requiring digestion; electrolytes replace sweat losses. Common endurance protocol.
pairings.dose: HBCD 30-60 g/h + EAAs 10-15 g + electrolytes per sweat rate.
pairings.source_field: HBCD entry: 'Can be combined with electrolytes and amino acids'

pairings.goal: Comprehensive B12 coverage (both active coenzyme forms)
pairings.rationale: Adenosyl-B12 is the mitochondrial coenzyme for methylmalonyl-CoA mutase; methyl-B12 is the cytosolic cofactor for methionine synthase. The body needs both; supplementing both avoids relying on intracellular conversion.
pairings.dose: Adeno-B12 1-5 mg + methyl-B12 1-5 mg, sublingual, daily or weekly.
pairings.source_field: Adenosylcobalamin entry: 'combine with methylcobalamin for comprehensive B12 coverage'

pairings.goal: Food-matrix vitamin C with capillary/vascular bioflavonoids
pairings.rationale: Food-matrix variant of p22. Acerola provides ascorbate in a bioflavonoid context, mirroring traditional European venotropic formulas.
pairings.dose: Acerola 500-1,000 mg (std. 17-25% C) + rutin 500 mg + hesperidin 500 mg + quercetin 500 mg.
pairings.source_field: Acerola entry: 'ascorbic acid alongside synergistic bioflavonoids including rutin, hesperidin, and quercetin'

pairings.rationale: Non-sedative, non-GABAergic stack: magnolia (anxiolytic via GABA-A modulation + cortisol), ashwagandha (cortisol reduction), theanine (alpha-waves). Often combined in pre-sleep formulas.
pairings.dose: Magnolia 200-400 mg + KSM-66 ashwagandha 300-600 mg + theanine 100-200 mg, 60 min pre-bed.
pairings.source_field: Magnolia bark entry: 'Commonly combined with ashwagandha or L-theanine in sleep formulas'

pairings.goal: Traditional TCM vitality / libido / cognition formula
pairings.rationale: Classical Chinese herbal pairing. Mechanisms are preliminary and human RCT data is limited; label as traditional-use.
pairings.dose: Cistanche 300-1,000 mg + epimedium 250-1,000 mg.
pairings.source_field: Cistanche entry: 'Often combined with Epimedium in traditional formulas'

pairings.rationale: Non-overlapping polyphenol targets; mostly additive. Clinical endpoints for the specific pair are thin.
pairings.dose: Dose not well-standardised; typically food-form or extract blend.
pairings.source_field: Protocatechuic acid entry: 'Pairs well with quercetin and other phenolics in a polyphenol stack'

pairings.rationale: The classical Duke dermatology stack for skin antioxidant protection; ferulic acid stabilises C and E in formulation and extends photoprotective effect. Topical evidence is strongest; oral synergy extrapolated.
pairings.dose: Topical serum: ferulic acid 0.5% + L-ascorbic acid 15% + tocopherol 1%. Oral: ferulic acid 250 mg + C 500 mg + E 15-30 mg mixed tocopherols.
pairings.source_field: Ferulic acid entry: 'synergistic UV protection and anti-aging combination'

pairings.rationale: Phytosterols displace cholesterol at intestinal absorption; beta-glucan sequesters bile acids. Different mechanisms; FDA recognises both as authorised cholesterol-lowering health claims. Additive ~5-10% each.
pairings.dose: Phytosterols 2 g/day (with fat-containing meal) + beta-glucan 3-10 g/day.
pairings.source_field: Phytosterols entry (fat requirement) + Oat beta-glucan entry

pairings.rationale: Ornithine and arginine recycle through the urea cycle; the combination shows larger GH responses in older trials than either alone. Modern practice often prefers L-citrulline for the NO endpoint.
pairings.dose: L-Arg 3-6 g + L-Orn 2-5 g, pre-bed.
pairings.source_field: L-Ornithine entry: 'Often combined with L-arginine for growth hormone release'

pairings.rationale: Pantethine modestly lowers LDL and TG; omega-3 targets TG; phytosterols lower LDL. Non-overlapping mechanisms for combined lipid panels.
pairings.dose: Pantethine 600-900 mg + EPA/DHA 1-2 g + phytosterols 2 g.
pairings.source_field: Pantethine entry: 'Can be combined with omega-3s or plant sterols for additional cholesterol support'

pairings.goal: Estrogen metabolism / Phase II conjugation support
pairings.rationale: Ca-D-glucarate inhibits beta-glucuronidase (reducing estrogen reabsorption); DIM shifts hydroxylation toward less-active 2-OH estrogens. Popular combo in functional-medicine estrogen-clearance protocols; direct combo RCTs are limited.
pairings.dose: Ca-D-glucarate 500-1,000 mg + DIM 100-200 mg.
pairings.source_field: Calcium D-glucarate entry: 'Sometimes combined with DIM for estrogen metabolism support'

pairings.rationale: D-Mannose blocks E. coli FimH adhesin at the bladder wall; cranberry PACs also inhibit bacterial adherence via a related mechanism. Both are listed in UTI guidelines; combination is additive.
pairings.dose: D-Mannose 2 g 1-2x/day + cranberry extract 500 mg (std. PACs 36 mg).

pairings.rationale: Both are substrates/cofactors for collagen synthesis. Lysine is the limiting amino acid for collagen cross-links; C is the cofactor. Cardiovascular-endpoint claims are unproven, but the connective-tissue rationale is sound.
pairings.dose: L-Lysine 1-3 g + Vitamin C 500-1,000 mg.

pairings.rationale: Maqui delphinidins have small dry-eye trials; lutein/zeaxanthin are the AREDS2 pigments. Independent evidence bases; combination not RCT-tested.
pairings.dose: Maqui 60-120 mg + lutein 10 mg + zeaxanthin 2 mg.

pairings.rationale: Insulin drives creatine transport into muscle. Taking creatine with protein + carbs (post-workout or meal) raises muscle creatine accumulation vs fasted dosing.
pairings.dose: Creatine 3-5 g + whey 20-30 g + 30-50 g carbohydrate, post-workout.
pairings.source_field: Creatine and whey entries; carb-insulin pairing is standard performance-nutrition practice

pairings.rationale: Whey is rapidly digested and spikes plasma amino acids — ideal post-workout to drive an acute MPS response. Casein clots in the stomach and releases amino acids slowly over ~7 h — ideal pre-bed to suppress overnight proteolysis. Layering the two by timing (not by mixing) covers both the post-exercise anabolic window and the overnight fasted period.
pairings.dose: Whey 20-30 g within 1-2 h post-workout + casein 30-40 g 30-60 min pre-bed on training days.
pairings.source_field: Casein protein entry: 'Casein before bed beats whey for overnight muscle building'

pairings.rationale: KSM-66 ashwagandha lowers cortisol and supports recovery from chronic stress; Rhodiola rosea is a stimulating adaptogen that supports energy and mental endurance. Complementary mechanisms (GABAergic/HPA-axis dampening + monoaminergic/fatigue resistance). No head-to-head RCT of the combination, but both have independent evidence and the combination is in clinical use.
pairings.dose: KSM-66 ashwagandha 300-600 mg/day (food, AM or PM) + Rhodiola rosea 200-400 mg/day (empty stomach, AM only). Cycle 8-12 weeks on, 2-4 weeks off.
pairings.source_field: Explicit 'Adaptogen stack' entry in data.js

pairings.rationale: Oral HA contributes to synovial fluid viscosity and joint lubrication; chondroitin supports cartilage GAG matrix and modestly reduces joint pain. A 2024 RCT of the combination found reduced knee stiffness and improved mobility at 3 months. Either alone is modest; combined evidence is the entry's basis.
pairings.dose: HA 80-200 mg/day (low-MW oral) + chondroitin sulfate 400-800 mg/day, with food. Allow 3 months.
pairings.source_field: Explicit 'Hyaluronic acid + Chondroitin stack' entry in data.js

pairings.goal: Energy and focus without tolerance buildup or cardiovascular stimulation
pairings.rationale: Distinct from p5 (L-theanine + caffeine). Theacrine acts on adenosine and dopaminergic pathways with minimal HR/BP effect and shows no tolerance even after 8 weeks of daily use. Combining with caffeine extends effective duration and broadens the neurochemical hit without the jitter or tolerance penalty. Useful when L-theanine has been tried and the user wants a longer-duration option.
pairings.dose: Theacrine 100-200 mg + caffeine 50-150 mg, morning or early afternoon. Avoid PM dosing.
pairings.source_field: Theacrine entry: 'Can be combined with caffeine for synergistic effect without jitters'

pairings.rationale: Proline (~28% of collagen residues) and glycine (~33%) are the two dominant amino acids in the collagen triple helix; vitamin C is the obligate cofactor for prolyl/lysyl hydroxylases. Supplementing all three together provides both substrate and cofactor and is more rationale-driven than collagen + C alone (p4) for users who do not consume collagen peptides.
pairings.dose: L-Proline 500-2,000 mg + Vitamin C 500 mg + Glycine 3-10 g daily.
pairings.source_field: L-Proline entry: 'alongside vitamin C and glycine for collagen synthesis support'

pairings.rationale: Independent and additive ergogenic mechanisms. Beta-alanine raises muscle carnosine, buffering H+ during 1-10 min efforts. Creatine raises phosphocreatine, supporting <30 s maximal efforts and total work output. Stacking covers both ATP-PCr and glycolytic energy systems. Multiple meta-analyses support each individually; combination is standard sports-nutrition practice.
pairings.dose: Beta-alanine 3.2-6.4 g/day (split or sustained-release) + Creatine monohydrate 3-5 g/day. Beta-alanine requires 4-6 weeks loading.
pairings.source_field: Carnosyn beta-alanine entry: 'Stacks well with creatine'

pairings.rationale: Classical TCM cardiovascular pairing (often as 'Compound Danshen Dripping Pill'). Both have anti-platelet and vasodilatory activity in lab and Chinese clinical studies; independent replication outside China is limited. Listed for completeness — additive bleeding risk is the dominant practical consideration.
pairings.dose: Dan Shen 1,500-3,000 mg/day + Sanqi 500-1,500 mg/day standardised extract.
pairings.source_field: Dan Shen compound entry: 'traditionally combined with Sanqi (Panax notoginseng)'

pairings.rationale: Creatine increases short-burst phosphocreatine; beta-alanine raises muscle carnosine to buffer H+ in 1-4 min efforts. Loaded together cover overlapping intensity zones.
pairings.dose: Creatine 3-5 g/day + Beta-alanine 3.2-6.4 g/day in divided 800 mg doses to minimise paresthesia.

pairings.rationale: Divalent cations (Ca2+, Mg2+, Zn2+) compete for the same divalent metal transporters (DMT-1) in the intestine. Doses >800 mg of any one significantly reduce uptake of the others.
pairings.dose: Take large mineral doses (>500 mg) at least 2 hours apart. Smaller everyday doses can usually be co-ingested without major loss.

pairings.rationale: Selective fermentation of acacia by Lactobacillus enhances strain persistence and short-chain-fatty-acid production.
pairings.dose: 1-10 billion CFU LGG + 5-15 g acacia, separately or in same dose with cool water (heat kills probiotics).

pairings.rationale: Bromelain enzymes increase intestinal absorption of quercetin; both have additive mast-cell stabilizing effects in allergy/inflammation.
pairings.dose: Quercetin 500 mg + Bromelain 100-250 mg, on empty stomach.

pairings.rationale: Independent meta-analyses show modest cold-symptom reduction; combining at first symptom is the most-studied protocol.
pairings.dose: Vitamin C 1000 mg + Zinc lozenge 13-23 mg every 2 hours within 24 h of symptom onset (max 5 days).

pairings.rationale: Two RCTs (Fathizadeh 2010; De Souza 2000) show greater PMS symptom reduction with Mg + B6 vs Mg alone.
pairings.dose: Magnesium 200-360 mg + P5P 40-80 mg from luteal phase through menstruation.

pairings.rationale: Phosphatidylcholine + DHA together build neuronal membranes; large prenatal cohorts find compounded cognitive benefits in offspring.
pairings.dose: Choline 450-550 mg + DHA 200-300 mg/day during pregnancy and lactation.

pairings.rationale: Tannins (tea/coffee), divalent cations, and casein phosphopeptides each significantly reduce non-heme iron uptake.
pairings.dose: Take iron on empty stomach with vitamin C; separate from calcium, zinc, magnesium, dairy, tea, coffee by 2 hours.

pairings.rationale: Magnesium is a cofactor for the enzymes converting vitamin D to its active form. Vitamin D supplementation increases magnesium needs.
pairings.dose: Vitamin D3 1000-2000 IU + Magnesium 200-400 mg/day with food.

pairings.rationale: Berberine activates AMPK; bergamot inhibits HMG-CoA reductase via brutieridin/melitidin. Italian RCTs combine for additive LDL reduction.
pairings.dose: Berberine 500 mg 2-3×/day + Bergamot 500-1000 mg/day with meals.

pairings.rationale: Citrulline boosts NO/blood flow during reps; beta-alanine buffers H+ for the 1-4 min ATP-PC + glycolytic crossover.
pairings.dose: Citrulline malate 6-8 g + Beta-alanine 3.2 g, 30-45 min pre-workout.

pairings.rationale: AREDS2 + LUTEGA trials: omega-3 enhances retinal carotenoid uptake. Effect on macular pigment optical density is greater than either alone.
pairings.dose: Lutein 10 mg + Zeaxanthin 2 mg + DHA 350 mg/day with a fatty meal.

pairings.rationale: Iodine is the substrate; selenium-dependent iodothyronine deiodinases convert T4 to T3 and protect the gland from peroxide damage.
pairings.dose: Selenium 100-200 mcg + Iodine 150 mcg/day. Higher iodine without selenium can worsen autoimmune thyroiditis.

pairings.rationale: Glycine lowers core body temperature; magnesium activates GABA receptors. Bambico/Inagawa trials show subjective sleep improvement.
pairings.dose: Glycine 3 g + Magnesium glycinate 300-400 mg, 30-60 min before bed.

pairings.rationale: Both raise GABA tone via different routes (theanine ionotropic; magnesium NMDA antagonism + GABA-A modulation).
pairings.dose: L-Theanine 200 mg + Magnesium 200-300 mg, evening or under acute stress.

pairings.rationale: Both modulate inflammation + monoamine signalling; combined saffron-curcumin has equivalent efficacy to fluoxetine 20 mg in mild-moderate depression (Akhondzadeh trial).
pairings.dose: Saffron 30 mg + Curcumin 500-1000 mg/day; 8-12 week trial.

pairings.goal: Magnesium uptake + cofactor activation for ~300 enzymes
pairings.rationale: B6 (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) is required for many magnesium-dependent enzymes; trials show greater PMS, anxiety, and migraine benefit when paired vs. magnesium alone.
pairings.dose: Magnesium glycinate 200-400 mg + P5P 25-50 mg/day, evening with food.

pairings.goal: Bone deposition triad — calcium absorbed (D3), directed to bone (K2), enzymes activated (Mg)
pairings.rationale: Magnesium is the cofactor that activates vitamin D and converts inactive osteocalcin; K2 then directs the calcium D3 absorbs into bone instead of arteries.
pairings.dose: D3 1000-2000 IU + MK-7 100-200 mcg + Mg glycinate 200-400 mg, with the largest meal.

pairings.rationale: Calcium reduces non-heme iron absorption by up to 60% via DMT-1 competition. Even ~165 mg calcium with iron blocks half the iron uptake.
pairings.dose: Separate iron and calcium doses by ≥2 hours (and avoid dairy with iron).

pairings.rationale: Berberine improves insulin sensitivity but is hepatically processed; silymarin protects hepatocytes during chronic use. Italian RCTs (Berberol K) combine for NAFLD and metabolic syndrome.
pairings.dose: Berberine 500 mg 2-3×/day + Silymarin 200-400 mg/day with meals.

pairings.rationale: Largest meta-analysis (Morton 2018) shows protein + creatine adds ~30% more LBM gain than protein alone in trained subjects.
pairings.dose: Whey 0.4 g/kg per meal + Creatine 3-5 g/day, post-workout convenient but timing not critical.

pairings.rationale: Prebiotic fibre selectively feeds the live bacteria; combined synbiotics outperform probiotics alone for IBS, immune, and metabolic outcomes in several meta-analyses.
pairings.dose: Multi-strain probiotic 5-30 billion CFU + inulin/FOS 5-10 g/day. Start low to avoid bloating.

pairings.rationale: Both required for the methionine cycle; deficiency in either causes hyperhomocysteinemia and demyelination. The methylated forms bypass MTHFR and MTRR polymorphisms.
pairings.dose: 5-MTHF 400-800 mcg + Methylcobalamin 1000 mcg sublingual daily, AM.

pairings.rationale: Lactoferrin enhances iron uptake while reducing GI side effects; vitamin C still aids reduction of Fe3+ → Fe2+. Useful in pregnancy and IBD trials.
pairings.dose: Ferrous bisglycinate 25-50 mg + Vitamin C 100-250 mg + Lactoferrin 100-200 mg, on empty stomach.

pairings.rationale: Three different GABAergic / cooling routes converge: glycine lowers core body temp, magnesium activates GABA-A, theanine modulates inhibitory tone via alpha-waves.
pairings.dose: Mg glycinate 300-400 mg + Glycine 3 g + L-Theanine 200 mg, 30-60 min before bed.

pairings.rationale: Magnesium is a required cofactor for the hepatic and renal hydroxylases that activate vitamin D. Mg-deficient subjects show poor 25-OH-D response to D3 supplementation.
pairings.dose: Mg 300-400 mg/day with D3 ≥1,000 IU; both with food.

pairings.rationale: Zinc downregulates intestinal copper absorption via metallothionein. Chronic zinc >25-30 mg/day without copper causes anemia and neuropathy.
pairings.dose: If Zn ≥25 mg/day, add Cu 1-2 mg/day. Optimal ratio ~8-15:1 Zn:Cu.

pairings.rationale: Ascorbate reduces ferric to ferrous iron in the gut, increasing absorption ~2-3×. Especially helpful with plant-based iron sources.
pairings.dose: Iron 30-60 mg + Vitamin C 100-250 mg, taken together on empty stomach if tolerated.

pairings.rationale: All three are required for normal RBC production; deficiency in any one limits the others' effect. Empirical iron without verifying B12/folate misses dimorphic anemias.
pairings.dose: Test ferritin + B12 + folate before dosing; correct deficits per labs.

pairings.rationale: All three are required cofactors in homocysteine remethylation and transsulfuration. Single-vitamin deficits will not normalize Hcy alone.
pairings.dose: B6 25-50 mg + B12 500-1,000 mcg methylcobalamin + 5-MTHF 400-800 mcg, daily.

pairings.rationale: B6 facilitates Mg transport into cells; combined Mg-B6 outperforms Mg alone in PMS, anxiety, and cramp endpoints in several RCTs.
pairings.dose: Mg 300 mg + B6 25-50 mg daily.

pairings.rationale: Vitamin E quenches lipid peroxyl radicals; CoQ10 (and vitamin C) regenerate tocopheroxyl radicals back to active vitamin E. The pair maintains the lipid antioxidant cycle.
pairings.dose: CoQ10 100-200 mg + mixed tocopherols 100-200 IU with fat.

pairings.rationale: Iodine is the substrate for T3/T4; selenium is the cofactor for deiodinases that activate T4→T3 and for glutathione-peroxidase that protects the thyroid from peroxide damage.
pairings.dose: Iodine ≤150 mcg/day (avoid in autoimmune thyroid) + Selenium 100-200 mcg selenomethionine.

pairings.rationale: D3 increases gut Ca absorption; K2 activates osteocalcin (bone) and MGP (vascular protection), directing Ca to bone rather than arteries. Mg is also required for D activation.
Choline, B12, and folate share the methyl pool. Low choline status raises folate/B12 demand and vice versa. Pregnancy demands all three.
pairings.dose: Choline 425-550 mg + B12 + folate per cycle requirements.

pairings.rationale: Glutathione = γ-glutamyl-cysteinyl-glycine. NAC supplies cysteine; glycine supplies the third amino acid. Older adults are typically deficient in BOTH substrates; supplementing one alone gives diminishing returns.
pairings.dose: Glycine 100 mg/kg + NAC 100 mg/kg daily, divided BID.

pairings.rationale: ALCAR shuttles fatty acids into mitochondria for β-oxidation; ALA recycles antioxidants and is a mitochondrial cofactor; CoQ10 carries electrons in the ETC. The triad supports mitochondrial bioenergetics from substrate to electron transport.
pairings.dose: ALCAR 1-2 g + ALA 600 mg + CoQ10 100-200 mg daily.

pairings.rationale: Whey provides the full EAA spectrum; leucine drives mTORC1 to peak; creatine provides the energetic substrate for high-power work. Older adults need ~3 g leucine to hit the synthesis threshold.
pairings.dose: Whey 25-40 g + Creatine 3-5 g + Leucine 2-3 g around training/major meals.

pairings.rationale: Vitamin C is required for prolyl/lysyl hydroxylase, the enzymes that stabilize collagen triple helix. Collagen amino-acid supply without vitamin C produces under-hydroxylated, unstable collagen.
pairings.dose: Collagen 10-20 g + Vitamin C 50-100 mg ~1 hour pre-loading-activity (Shaw 2017 protocol).

pairings.rationale: Each has independent evidence; the trio addresses mitochondrial energy production failure thought to underlie migraine. Riboflavin 400 mg, Mg 400-600 mg, CoQ10 100 mg each have their own RCT support.
pairings.dose: Riboflavin 400 mg + Mg 400 mg + CoQ10 100 mg daily for ≥3 months.

pairings.rationale: Calcium competitively inhibits both heme and non-heme iron absorption at any dose. The effect is large enough to cause clinical iron deficiency in heavy calcium users.
pairings.dose: Separate by ≥2 hours.

pairings.rationale: Iron and zinc share intestinal divalent metal transporters. Co-dosing reduces zinc absorption by 30-50% and iron absorption by similar amounts.
pairings.dose: Separate by ≥2 hours.

pairings.rationale: At doses ≥500 mg, calcium and magnesium compete for absorption transporters. Smaller doses (≤250 mg each) co-administered are less affected.
pairings.dose: Separate doses ≥1,000 mg by ≥2 hours; smaller co-doses are fine.

pairings.rationale: Coffee/tea polyphenols (chlorogenic acid, tannins) bind iron in the gut, reducing non-heme iron absorption by 60-90% when co-consumed. Effect persists ~1 hour after the beverage.
pairings.dose: Separate iron from coffee/tea by ≥1 hour either side.

pairings.goal: Modest calcium loss with high caffeine
pairings.rationale: Each 100 mg caffeine increases urinary calcium loss by ~5 mg. Effect is small but adds up across high coffee/energy drink consumption.
pairings.dose: If high caffeine intake, ensure adequate calcium status.

pairings.rationale: High-dose isolated B6 can outcompete B5 absorption when taken together. Balanced B-complex avoids this.
pairings.dose: Use balanced B-complex rather than mega-doses of single Bs.

pairings.rationale: High-dose niacin (≥1 g/day) consumes methyl groups for methyl-niacinamide excretion, depleting SAM-e. Adding methyl donors (folate, B12, choline) prevents the homocysteine elevation seen with chronic high-dose niacin.
pairings.dose: If on niacin ≥1 g/day, add 5-MTHF 800 mcg + B12 1 mg + choline 500 mg.

pairings.rationale: Soy isoflavones inhibit thyroid peroxidase. Iodine repletion mitigates the effect. In iodine-deficient individuals, high-soy + low-iodine diets are linked to hypothyroidism.
pairings.dose: If consuming high soy, ensure iodine status is adequate (urine spot check).

pairings.rationale: Levothyroxine absorption is blunted by minerals (Ca, Fe, Mg, Al), soy, fiber, and coffee. Separate by ≥4 hours from any of these.
pairings.dose: Take levothyroxine 30-60 min before food/coffee; separate Ca/Fe/Mg/soy by ≥4 h.

pairings.rationale: Caffeine is renally excreted with small magnesium loss; high caffeine + Mg-deficiency state correlates with worse sleep. Take Mg evening, caffeine morning.
pairings.dose: Mg evening; caffeine before noon.

pairings.goal: Avoid antimicrobial blunting of probiotic strains
pairings.rationale: Berberine has broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity that can reduce probiotic viability when co-administered. Separate dosing windows preserves both effects.
pairings.dose: Separate by ≥4 hours.

pairings.rationale: Carvacrol/thymol in oregano oil have antibacterial activity against probiotic strains. Separation preserves both.
pairings.dose: Separate by ≥4 hours.

pairings.rationale: L-theanine increases alpha-wave activity and blunts the sympathetic spike from caffeine without reducing the cognitive boost. The 1:2 caffeine:theanine ratio is best-studied.
pairings.dose: Caffeine 100 mg + L-theanine 200 mg.

pairings.rationale: Caffeine raises sympathetic tone; ashwagandha lowers it. Co-dosing tends to cancel the calming effect of ashwagandha.
pairings.dose: If using ashwagandha for stress reduction, take it on the opposite end of the day from caffeine.

pairings.goal: Avoid mutual antagonism at high doses
pairings.rationale: At extreme doses, retinol and D3 can antagonize each other's nuclear receptor effects. At normal supplemental doses (D3 ≤2,000 IU; retinol ≤3,000 IU), this is rarely clinically meaningful.
pairings.dose: Avoid simultaneous mega-doses; balanced AD3K formulas are fine.

pairings.goal: Iron deficit recovery while on bone protocol
pairings.rationale: Bone-protocol calcium doses (1,000+ mg/day) heavily blunt iron absorption. Heavy menstruating women on calcium often present with iron deficiency despite oral iron.
pairings.dose: Iron AM, fasting; calcium with lunch/dinner; separate by ≥4h.

pairings.rationale: Hot beverages (≥50°C) directly damage non-spore probiotic strains. Take with cool/room-temp water.
pairings.dose: Don't swallow probiotics with hot coffee/tea.

pairings.rationale: Caffeine half-life is 5-7 hours; even afternoon coffee blunts melatonin's sleep-onset effect. Last caffeine should be ~8 hours before intended sleep.
pairings.dose: Caffeine cutoff 14:00 if sleeping by 22:00.

pairings.rationale: Tea and coffee tannins reduce zinc absorption by ~30-50% when co-consumed. Less severe than the iron interaction but real.
pairings.dose: Separate zinc from coffee/tea by ≥1 hour.

pairings.rationale: Soluble fibre slows gastric emptying. For overnight casein-style sustained release this is fine; for fast post-workout absorption, separate them.
pairings.dose: If post-workout fast absorption is the goal, take whey alone; otherwise co-dosing is fine.

pairings.rationale: Oral bisphosphonates have <1% bioavailability and any mineral or food blocks them entirely. Take FIRST THING with plain water; no supplements/food/coffee for 30-60 min.
pairings.dose: Bisphosphonate FIRST THING with water; supplements ≥60 min later.

pairings.rationale: Both can cause mild GI upset; co-dosing on empty stomach amplifies it. Take with food or separate.
pairings.dose: Take with food.

pairings.rationale: Same as berberine + probiotics caution above; separation by ≥4h preserves both effects.
pairings.dose: Separate by ≥4 hours.

pairings.rationale: Mega-doses of vitamin C can degrade B12 in vitro; in practice, in-vivo effect at typical supplement doses is small.
pairings.dose: Separate B12 from mega-dose vitamin C by ≥1 hour.

pairings.rationale: Lutein and zeaxanthin are fat-soluble; absorption is 3-5× higher when co-ingested with fat (≥10 g).
pairings.dose: Take with breakfast containing eggs/avocado/nuts.

pairings.rationale: Each item has antiplatelet or fibrinolytic activity at supplement doses. Stacking 3+ adds clinically meaningful bleeding risk; combined with anticoagulants, risk becomes serious.
pairings.dose: Limit to 1-2 of these at a time; flag any to anticoagulation clinic.

pairings.rationale: Each can cause idiosyncratic hepatotoxicity. Stacking 2+ multiplies risk. Add alcohol or acetaminophen and risk increases further.
pairings.dose: Avoid combining; rotate or eliminate.

pairings.goal: Serotonin syndrome risk — AVOID stacking, AVOID with serotonergic Rx
pairings.rationale: Each raises serotonin via different mechanisms. Stacking elevates risk; co-administration with SSRI/SNRI/MAOI is dangerous.
pairings.dose: Use one at a time; never combine with serotonergic prescription drugs.

pairings.rationale: Each has modest glucose-lowering effects. Stacking + insulin/sulfonylurea can cause hypoglycemia. Otherwise safe when stacked in non-diabetic prediabetes contexts under monitoring.
pairings.dose: If on insulin/sulfonylurea, monitor glucose closely when adding any of these.

pairings.rationale: Each has mild BP-lowering effect. Stacking 3+ can produce additive hypotension, especially in those already on antihypertensives.
pairings.dose: Monitor BP; reduce antihypertensive dose only under prescriber guidance.

pairings.goal: CNS depression additive — caution in OSA, on benzo/opioids
pairings.rationale: Sedating supplements stack additively. Combined with benzodiazepines, opioids, alcohol, or untreated sleep apnea, the additive effect can be dangerous.
pairings.dose: Avoid combining sedating supplements with sedating prescription drugs or alcohol.

pairings.goal: Sympathomimetic additive — AVOID in CV disease, AFib
pairings.rationale: Each raises heart rate / BP / sympathetic tone via different mechanisms. Stacking causes palpitations, hypertension, and (in vulnerable individuals) arrhythmia.
pairings.dose: Avoid combining; especially avoid in CVD, AFib, hypertension, anxiety disorders.

pairings.goal: Estrogenic additive — caution in oestrogen-sensitive cancers
pairings.rationale: Phyto-oestrogenic supplements stack. Useful in symptomatic perimenopause; risky in oestrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer history. DIM is anti-oestrogenic but in the same hormonal pathway.
pairings.dose: Discuss with oncologist if any history of ER+ breast cancer.

pairings.goal: BPH symptom relief — additive for symptom control
pairings.rationale: Each has small but real effect on urinary symptoms via 5-alpha reductase / inflammatory pathways. Stacking improves symptom score modestly but does NOT replace 5-ARI prescription drugs in moderate-severe BPH.
pairings.dose: Saw palmetto 320 mg + Beta-sitosterol 60-130 mg + Pygeum 100 mg + Pumpkin seed oil 320 mg, daily.

pairings.rationale: NAC + glycine give substrate; vitamin C recycles oxidized glutathione (GSSG → GSH). The triad is more effective than NAC alone for raising tissue glutathione.
pairings.dose: NAC 600-1,200 mg + Glycine 3-5 g + Vitamin C 500 mg, divided BID.

medication_depletion_pairings.reason: Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase (shared with CoQ10 synthesis)
Bone density loss and Zn depletion
Restore gut flora; take 2+ h apart from the antibiotic dose
Loop diuretics increase urinary loss of Mg, K, and thiamine; chronic use causes deficiency.
Thiazides cause urinary loss of Mg, K, and Zn.
Some ACE inhibitors (captopril) increase urinary zinc; chronic use can cause taste disturbance and zinc deficiency.
OCPs deplete B6, folate, B12, Mg, Zn — relevant for both contraceptive use and pre-conception planning when stopping OCPs.
Isoniazid is a B6 antagonist; B6 supplementation is standard to prevent peripheral neuropathy.
Chronic steroids cause bone loss and Ca/Mg/K losses.
Methotrexate is a folate antagonist; daily folate supplementation reduces side effects without blunting therapeutic effect.
Acid suppression reduces absorption of B12 (cyanocobalamin form), magnesium, calcium, and non-heme iron.
Bile-acid sequestrants reduce absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.

avoid_pairings_absorption.reason: Polyphenols and calcium block Fe absorption
Thyroid-hormone absorption is blocked by minerals
Fibre binds drugs in the gut

members: Ginkgo biloba / Ginger / high-dose Omega-3 / Curcumin / Nattokinase / Lumbrokinase / Dan Shen / Dong quai / Red sage / White willow / Meadowsweet

dangerous_pairings.risk: Thyroid disruption, excessive sedation, immune counteraction

members: Synephrine (bitter orange) / Ephedra analogues / Yohimbine / DMAA / DMHA

dangerous_pairings.risk: Fatal serotonin syndrome — methylene blue is a potent MAO-A inhibitor; FDA black-box-equivalent warning.
Hypertensive crisis; banned combination in psychiatric care.
Cardiovascular events including MI, stroke, sudden cardiac death (Ephedra-caffeine combos were banned by FDA in 2004 for this reason).
Severe CNS depression, respiratory depression, coma. Phenibut also causes withdrawal seizures with abrupt cessation after chronic use.
Respiratory depression, opioid-like withdrawal, addiction; tianeptine has mu-opioid agonism at supra-therapeutic doses sold in supplements.
Mu-opioid + benzodiazepine combination: respiratory depression and overdose.
DNP uncouples mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation; lethal hyperthermia at supra-supplement doses. NOT a supplement; sold illegally for weight loss; multiple confirmed deaths annually.
Antagonizes warfarin at any meaningful dose; can drop INR rapidly and cause thrombosis. NEVER add or remove without anticoagulation-clinic supervision.
